objective: Let $f(z)=\prod_{i=1}^n(z-z_i)\in \mathbb{C}[z]$ with $\lvert z_i\rvert < 1$ for all $i$. Must there always exist a path of length less than $2$ in\[\{z: \lvert f(z)\rvert < 1\}\]which connects two of the roots of $f$?
completion_criteria: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: The original 1958 statement, strict inequalities, current open status, closest recent work, failed gradient-flow route, and Lean semantics were audited. The inherited Terra script was found to test unnormalized products and omit the degree-dependent separation condition, so its reported degree 4-7 samples were rejected. A corrected deterministic script with analytic, quartic, invariance, and dense-grid controls accepted 32 degree-five configurations after 951 attempts and found no failure. The largest second-smallest arm maximum was 0.9992275819269636. This is not a proof. The required Formal Conjectures bootstrap failed twice for lack of decompression space; invalid generated caches were removed and 5.6 GB free space restored.

established_facts.claim: EHP state that some component of {|f|<1} contains at least two zeros.
established_facts.evidence: Original paper, page 139 immediately above Problem 5.

established_facts.claim: A root pair at distance d<2^((4-n)/2) is connected by its straight segment inside {|f|<1}.
established_facts.evidence: Along the segment, the pair factors are at most d^2/4 and each remaining factor is strictly below 2.
established_facts.scope: Every degree n>=2 polynomial whose roots lie in the open unit disk.

established_facts.claim: No normalized radial-star failure occurred in the recorded 32-sample degree-five run.
established_facts.scope: Only the stated deterministic float64 samples and tolerance.

strategies.title: Mandatory source/status baseline followed by a controlled audit of the minimum-enclosing-disk radial-star route underlying the June 2026 quartic preprint.
strategies.mechanism: Normalize configurations by their minimum enclosing disk, enforce the complement of the elementary close-pair reduction, and compute the maximum product along every radial arm from stationary points of the squared-modulus polynomial.
strategies.hypothesis: For each of 32 deterministic degree-five samples whose normalized pair distances are at least 1/sqrt(2), at least two radial arms have dimensionless product maximum at most 1 within the declared floating margin.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compute all normalized arm maxima; a configuration whose second-smallest maximum exceeds 1 rejects the sampled stronger radial lemma, while no such configuration establishes only finite-sample survival.
strategies.blocker: The mathematical blocker is a dimension-five selection lemma or a rigorously certified failure of it. The practical formalization blocker is insufficient temporary disk space for the pinned mathlib cache and the current target's incorrect unrestricted length semantics.
strategies.reopen_condition: Not applicable to the active radial strategy. For formal compilation, provide at least 8 GB free or a streaming cache bootstrap; for the flow-tree route, satisfy its explicit critical-network and length-estimate condition.

strategies.mechanism: Represent {|f|<1} as a real semialgebraic domain and certify barriers or variational lower bounds for all paths between specified roots.
strategies.hypothesis: A structured exact root configuration may admit a finite barrier certificate proving all relevant intrinsic distances are at least 2.
strategies.discriminating_test: On a toy quadratic or quartic instance with known short paths, require the encoding to return the correct lower bound or explicit violating path before applying it to candidates.
strategies.rationale: This is the certificate surface ultimately required for a genuine counterexample, unlike grid shortest paths.
strategies.title: Represent {|f|<1} as a real semialgebraic domain and certify barriers or variational lower bounds for all paths between specified roots.

strategies.mechanism: Replace range Hausdorff measure with parametrized variation/rectifiable length or restrict to polygonal paths with a proved equivalence.
strategies.hypothesis: A compiling, semantically equivalent statement can be expressed using existing mathlib curve-length infrastructure.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compile a retracing-path regression example where range measure is strictly smaller than variation, then show the repaired predicate distinguishes them.
strategies.rationale: It offers a bounded research-software contribution independent of solving the conjecture.
strategies.title: Replace range Hausdorff measure with parametrized variation/rectifiable length or restrict to polygonal paths with a proved equivalence.

open_leads.rationale: It directly extends the closest quartic method and has a cheap falsifier.
open_leads.next_experiment: Build the exact rational checker, then optimize the second-smallest arm maximum over a fixed adversarial family portfolio.

open_leads.rationale: It converts a cheap numerical lead into a rigorous scoped route elimination.
open_leads.next_experiment: Verify rational points, exact disk support, pair separation, and rational t witnesses using fraction arithmetic and polynomial sign checks.

open_leads.description: Correct the Formal Conjectures length semantics.
open_leads.rationale: This is an independently useful formalization/software contribution and prevents an invalid acceptance surface.
open_leads.next_experiment: After ensuring at least 8 GB free, rerun bootstrap and identify the mathlib rectifiable-curve or variation API.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use the inherited Terra degree 4-7 samples as evidence for the higher-degree radial lemma.
ruled_out.scope: The unlogged inherited script and its reported samples.
ruled_out.reason: It evaluated unnormalized products, omitted the relevant separation condition, and could suppress maxima through the missing scale factor.
ruled_out.evidence: Initial script SHA-256 b0034686b1bdd598a1b1ac052e721edb2ac19b3182e790b6a382b4fce6b1ff66 and the audited replacement note.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: None; use the corrected normalized implementation or a materially independent encoding.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Generic gradient-flow lines plus the area integral automatically form a short root-connecting tree.
ruled_out.scope: The March 2026 forum strategy relying on Proposition 12 and strip topology.
ruled_out.reason: Generic flow lines do not form the asserted tree, and removing a sufficiently large critical neighborhood destroys the length budget.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Provide an explicit critical-point connection rule and a quantitative length bound that works already for z^2-r^2.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the current Formal Conjectures file as a proof or exact formal acceptance target.
ruled_out.reason: Both theorems contain sorry and the length semantics undercount general retracing paths.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A successful no-sorry build with corrected parametrized-length semantics or a proved equivalence for the restricted path class.

next_session.objective: Adversarially test the degree-five normalized radial-star lemma with an exact certificate path ready.
next_session.first_action: Run `python3 tools/radial_star_discriminator.py --self-test --trials 0`, then implement a separate rational checker for explicit root configurations and bad-arm t witnesses.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop and exact-certify if the second-smallest maximum exceeds 1+1e-4; otherwise stop numerical work after the predefined adversarial families and one fixed-budget optimizer.
